deep_dive.title: Implicit Neural Representations of Individual Behavior
deep_dive.abstract: We study policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data. Each episode is generated by a fixed policy, but policy labels are unavailable. This setting appears in robotics play, demonstrations, games, racing, and other datasets where heterogeneous behaviors are mixed without annotations. We introduce \emph{Behavioral INR}, a self-supervised generative model that adapts implicit neural representations (INRs) from vision to behavior. Instead of mapping coordinates to RGB values, Behavioral INR represents a policy as a state-action function mapping states to subsequent actions. An episode-level latent modulates this function through FiLM layers, yielding a generative prior over policies and allowing policy identity to be inferred without supervision. Because INRs treat each datapoint as samples from an underlying function, the same model naturally accommodates variable episode lengths and different sampling granularities, as in vision INRs with different image resolutions. We also define policy-level out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts along state-distribution and action-distribution axes, which arise when policies overlap in states or actions but are not captured by standard behavioral OOD settings based only on new agents or environments. We evaluate on synthetic Gaussian random field data, MuJoCo demonstrations with controlled OOD splits, and real-world chess, Formula 1 racing, robotics, and Seek-Avoid datasets. Behavioral INR most consistently improves policy identifiability in the hardest continuous state-action settings, especially when longer episodes, more policies, and OOD splits reduce the usefulness of marginal shortcuts; amortized history encoders remain competitive when policy identity can be recovered from symbolic repetition or low-dimensional action statistics. We release code and checkpoints.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: player behavior simulation from unlabeled data
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从无标签数据中学习玩家行为模拟
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从无标签数据中学习玩家行为模拟
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从无标签数据中学习玩家行为模拟
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Behavioral INR, a self-supervised generative model for policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy data, applicable to games, robotics, and racing for player behavior modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有行为建模方法通常需要策略标签，但许多游戏数据集包含无标签的异构行为。本文提出Behavioral INR，一种自监督生成模型，将隐式神经表示从视觉扩展到行为，将策略表示为状态-动作函数。通过FiLM层调制，该模型能从无标签的多策略数据中学习策略先验，并推断策略身份。在游戏、机器人等场景中，该方法能够有效模拟玩家行为，为无监督的虚拟玩家建模提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有行为建模方法通常需要策略标签，但许多游戏数据集包含无标签的异构行为。本文提出Behavioral INR，一种自监督生成模型，将隐式神经表示从视觉扩展到行为，将策略表示为状态-动作函数。通过FiLM层调制，该模型能从无标签的多策略数据中学习策略先验，并推断策略身份。在游戏、机器人等场景中，该方法能够有效模拟玩家行为，为无监督的虚拟玩家建模提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: An Entropy-based Framework for Hybrid Coalitions in Game Theory. Part I: Human Arbitration
quick_skim.abstract: Classical Game Theory underpins much of AI and multiagent research, but hybrid Human AI systems require a framework in which execution authority can alternate within a digital environment. We introduce NeoGame Theory, an extension of classical Game Theory for hybrid Human AI coalitions operating under Virtual Nature, the algorithmic analogue of classical (physical) Nature. The framework combines a lexicographic coalition utility with a delegation rule based on the Jensen-Shannon divergence between Human and AI policies. Two thresholds define agreement, contextual, and disagreement regions. In the contextual region, execution follows a scenario specific rule. Apart from the theory, in this paper we develop the first regime, Human arbitration, in which the AI learns by observation and frequency matching while the Human retains final execution authority. We establish the axiomatic basis of the framework and characterize a frequency convergence equilibrium, providing the foundation for later extensions and computational validation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: game-theoretic framework for hybrid human-AI coalitions, relevant to AI playing games
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 人机混合联盟的博弈论框架，与AI玩游戏相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 人机混合联盟的博弈论框架，与AI玩游戏相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 人机混合联盟的博弈论框架，与AI玩游戏相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extends game theory for human-AI coalitions with delegation rules based on Jensen-Shannon divergence; applicable to AI in games.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对人机混合系统中执行权交替的需求，提出NeoGame理论，结合词典序效用和基于JS散度的委托规则，定义共识、情境和分歧区域。在人类仲裁机制下发展了第一体制，为游戏中人机协作决策提供了理论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对人机混合系统中执行权交替的需求，提出NeoGame理论，结合词典序效用和基于JS散度的委托规则，定义共识、情境和分歧区域。在人类仲裁机制下发展了第一体制，为游戏中人机协作决策提供了理论基础。

quick_skim.title: 3SPO: State-Score-Supervised Policy Optimization for LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents via reinforcement learning (RL) has enabled frontier models to achieve superhuman performance in long-horizon tasks. However, existing RL algorithms operate at the trajectory level, performing policy optimization only after collecting complete episode rollouts. This coarse-grained approach faces fundamental challenges in multi-turn agent settings where rewards are sparse, delayed, and credit assignment across individual steps is critical. In this work, we propose \textbf{State-Score-Supervised Policy Optimization (3SPO)}, a novel RL algorithm that performs post-step policy optimization with dynamic state score supervision. At each step, 3SPO computes the state score based on historical success rates, supervising step-wise credit assignment, adaptive rollout and post-step policy optimization without requiring value function estimation or additional auxiliary models. Theoretically, under a per-state bandit abstraction, we show that the proposed score-supervised allocation mechanism achieves logarithmic allocation regret and provide sample-complexity guarantees for action identification, score distinguishability, and filtering stability. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebShop with Qwen2.5-1.5B/7B-Instruct show that 3SPO consistently outperforms GRPO by $+22.6\%$ on ALFWorld and $+15.6$ points on WebShop, while using comparable resources to achieve $2.4\times$ more state exploration and $1.8\times$ faster convergence. Code is available at https://github.com/genalyu/3SPO.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes state-score-supervised policy optimization for LLM agents applicable to training in game environments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出状态分数监督策略优化用于LLM代理，可应用于游戏环境训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出状态分数监督策略优化用于LLM代理，可应用于游戏环境训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出状态分数监督策略优化用于LLM代理，可应用于游戏环境训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces 3SPO a RL algorithm that performs post-step policy optimization with dynamic state score supervision improving credit assignment in multi-turn agent tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对现有RL算法在稀疏延迟奖励的多轮代理设置中信用分配困难的问题，提出状态分数监督策略优化（3SPO）。该方法在每一步后基于历史成功率计算状态分数进行策略优化，实现细粒度信用分配。实验表明3SPO在长时任务中性能优于轨迹级RL算法，为游戏环境中智能体训练提供了有效方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该工作针对现有RL算法在稀疏延迟奖励的多轮代理设置中信用分配困难的问题，提出状态分数监督策略优化（3SPO）。该方法在每一步后基于历史成功率计算状态分数进行策略优化，实现细粒度信用分配。实验表明3SPO在长时任务中性能优于轨迹级RL算法，为游戏环境中智能体训练提供了有效方法。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Search for AI agent training in game environments

quick_skim.title: Should LLM Agents Decide in Social Simulations? Comparing Finite-State and LLM-Based Decision Policies
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as decision-making components in social simulations. This introduces a methodological risk: the simulation may deviate from the explicit behavioral policy defined by the researcher. In online social network (OSN) simulations, action choices shape system dynamics, interaction patterns, and model interpretability.   This paper evaluates whether LLM action selectors preserve an interpretable reference policy in an OSN simulation. The reference is a finite state machine implemented as a first-order Markov model, with transition probabilities depending on the user type. The evaluation uses a synthetic network with 1,000 agents and 10,000 action decisions. Three open-weight LLMs are tested: LLaMA 3.1, GPT-OSS, and Mistral 24B. Each model is evaluated under three prompting strategies: base, guided, and probabilistic. Alignment is measured using Jensen-Shannon Divergence with Laplace smoothing, and execution time is reported. Results show that LLMs can approximate the reference policy in some configurations, but do not preserve it reliably. Alignment varies across models and prompts, and additional guidance can introduce systematic action biases. Even the best-aligned LLM configurations are several hundred times slower than direct Markov chain sampling. These findings indicate that LLM-based action selection is not a direct replacement for explicit decision policies: it can alter the intended behavior while increasing computational cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: comparing LLM and FSM decision policies in agent simulations; applicable to player behavior modeling in games
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 比较LLM与有限状态机在代理模拟中的决策策略，可迁移至游戏玩家行为建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 比较LLM与有限状态机在代理模拟中的决策策略，可迁移至游戏玩家行为建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 比较LLM与有限状态机在代理模拟中的决策策略，可迁移至游戏玩家行为建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates whether LLM agents preserve interpretable reference policies in social simulations, proposing a comparison with finite-state machine decision policies; relevant for designing player behavior models in games.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对社交模拟中LLM代理决策偏离研究者预设行为策略的风险，提出以有限状态机（一阶马尔可夫模型）为参考策略，评估LLM动作选择器是否保持可解释性。实验采用1000个代理和10000个行动决策的合成社交网络。研究发现LLM决策可能偏离参考策略，提示在游戏玩家建模中需谨慎使用LLM直接决策。该方法为比较不同决策策略提供了框架，可用于评估游戏AI的行为一致性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对社交模拟中LLM代理决策偏离研究者预设行为策略的风险，提出以有限状态机（一阶马尔可夫模型）为参考策略，评估LLM动作选择器是否保持可解释性。实验采用1000个代理和10000个行动决策的合成社交网络。研究发现LLM决策可能偏离参考策略，提示在游戏玩家建模中需谨慎使用LLM直接决策。该方法为比较不同决策策略提供了框架，可用于评估游戏AI的行为一致性。

quick_skim.title: STAGE-Claw: Automated State-based Agent Benchmarking for Realistic Scenarios
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models are increasingly used to power personal agents for everyday applications, but evaluating these agents remains a challenge. Existing benchmarks still rely on sandboxed artifacts, static task design, and coarse scoring, which hinder scalability and limit progress toward reliable personal-agent evaluation. This paper introduces STAGE-Claw, an automated framework for building and evaluating realistic personal-agent scenarios in state-based personal-computing environments. Given a task hint, STAGE-Claw automatically creates and validates a realistic benchmark task with its environment, task prompts, ground truth, and related verification programs. Agents are then evaluated in realistic operating environments, where performance is measured by the correctness of the final system state rather than only the textual response. Using STAGE-Claw, this paper creates a benchmark with 40 challenging real scenario agent tasks, evaluates 11 frontier models, and analyzes their task scores, costs, tool-call reliability, and common failure patterns. Overall, STAGE-Claw offers a scalable, state-based way to evaluate agents in realistic user scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: automated benchmarking framework for agents in realistic scenarios applicable to games
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自动化智能体基准测试框架，适用于游戏场景
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自动化智能体基准测试框架，适用于游戏场景
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自动化智能体基准测试框架，适用于游戏场景
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: STAGE-Claw automates the creation and evaluation of realistic personal-agent scenarios, providing a framework that could be adapted for game agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有智能体基准依赖沙箱和静态任务的问题，本文提出STAGE-Claw自动化框架，能根据任务提示自动创建并验证真实场景的基准任务，包括环境、提示、真值及验证程序。该框架在状态性个人计算环境中评估智能体性能。虽然当前聚焦个人助手，但其方法可迁移至游戏智能体评估。该工作推进了智能体评估的自动化和真实化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有智能体基准依赖沙箱和静态任务的问题，本文提出STAGE-Claw自动化框架，能根据任务提示自动创建并验证真实场景的基准任务，包括环境、提示、真值及验证程序。该框架在状态性个人计算环境中评估智能体性能。虽然当前聚焦个人助手，但其方法可迁移至游戏智能体评估。该工作推进了智能体评估的自动化和真实化。
